 {"id":"bd-wsef","title":"Implement portable directory-based locking","description":"Implements portable directory-based locking for parallel processing.

## Parent Epic: bd-eta6 (Parallel Processing & Work Queue)

## Purpose
Provide atomic lock acquisition/release for coordinating parallel workers without race conditions.

## Implementation

### dir_lock_acquire()
```bash
# Acquire a directory-based lock
# Args: $1=lock_path, $2=timeout_seconds
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on timeout
dir_lock_acquire() {
    local lock_path="$1"
    local timeout="${2:-30}"
    local start_time=$(date +%s)

    while true; do
        # mkdir is atomic on POSIX
        if mkdir "$lock_path" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Write owner info for debugging stale locks
            echo "$$:$(date +%s)" > "$lock_path/owner"
            return 0
        fi

        # Check for stale lock (owner process dead)
        if [[ -f "$lock_path/owner" ]]; then
            local owner_pid owner_time
            IFS=':' read -r owner_pid owner_time < "$lock_path/owner"
            if ! kill -0 "$owner_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
                log_warn "Removing stale lock from dead PID $owner_pid"
                rm -rf "$lock_path"
                continue
            fi
            # Check for very old locks (>5 min = likely abandoned)
            local now=$(date +%s)
            if [[ $((now - owner_time)) -gt 300 ]]; then
                log_warn "Removing abandoned lock (age: $((now - owner_time))s)"
                rm -rf "$lock_path"
                continue
            fi
        fi

        # Check timeout
        local elapsed=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
        if [[ $elapsed -ge $timeout ]]; then
            return 1
        fi

        sleep 0.1
    done
}
```

### dir_lock_release()
```bash
# Release a directory-based lock
# Args: $1=lock_path
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 if not owner
dir_lock_release() {
    local lock_path="$1"

    # Verify we own the lock
    if [[ -f "$lock_path/owner" ]]; then
        local owner_pid
        IFS=':' read -r owner_pid _ < "$lock_path/owner"
        if [[ "$owner_pid" != "$$" ]]; then
            log_error "Attempted to release lock owned by PID $owner_pid"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    rm -rf "$lock_path"
    return 0
}
```

## Why Directory-Based Locks
- mkdir is atomic on POSIX systems
- Works across all shells without flock dependency
- Can store metadata (owner PID, timestamp)
- Visible for debugging (can ls the lock)

## Lock Locations
- ${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks/queue.lock - Work queue access
- ${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks/results.lock - Results file access
- ${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks/backoff.lock - Rate limit state access

## Stale Lock Handling
- Check if owner PID is still alive
- Auto-remove locks older than 5 minutes
- Log when removing stale locks

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lock acquisition is atomic (no race conditions)
- [ ] Stale locks from dead processes are cleaned up
- [ ] Timeout works correctly
- [ ] Lock release verifies ownership
- [ ] Works on Linux and macOS","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:35:50.952186887-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:25:34.798348164-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:25:34.798348164-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - dir_lock_try_acquire, dir_lock_release, dir_lock_acquire at lines 292-321"}
 {"id":"bd-wu0c","title":"Create ntm mock script for testing","description":"# ntm Mock Script for Testing

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Purpose
Enable E2E testing without real ntm/tmux/Claude sessions.

## Mock Script Location
scripts/test_bin/ntm (added to PATH during tests)

## Scenario-Based Responses

Set NTM_MOCK_SCENARIO to control behavior:
- ok: Happy path with valid commit plan (default)
- ok_with_release: Happy path including release plan
- timeout: Wait timeout
- resource_busy: Session already exists
- agent_error: Agent crashes
- rate_limited: Rate limit detected
- spawn_fail: Spawn fails
- invalid_json: Agent produces malformed JSON
- no_markers: Agent output missing markers

## Implementation

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
scenario="${NTM_MOCK_SCENARIO:-ok}"

case "$1" in
    --robot-status)
        echo "{\"success\":true,\"sessions\":[]}"
        ;;
    --robot-spawn=*)
        if [[ "$scenario" == "resource_busy" ]]; then
            echo "{\"success\":false,\"error_code\":\"RESOURCE_BUSY\"}"
            exit 1
        fi
        if [[ "$scenario" == "spawn_fail" ]]; then
            echo "{\"success\":false,\"error_code\":\"INTERNAL_ERROR\"}"
            exit 1
        fi
        echo "{\"success\":true,\"session\":\"test\",\"agents\":[{\"pane\":\"0.1\",\"ready\":true}]}"
        ;;
    --robot-send=*)
        # Store the prompt for simulating response
        echo "${*#*--msg=}" > /tmp/ntm_mock_last_prompt
        echo "{\"success\":true,\"delivered\":1}"
        ;;
    --robot-wait=*)
        if [[ "$scenario" == "timeout" ]]; then
            echo "{\"success\":false,\"error_code\":\"TIMEOUT\"}"
            exit 1
        fi
        if [[ "$scenario" == "agent_error" ]]; then
            echo "{\"success\":false,\"error_code\":\"INTERNAL_ERROR\"}"
            exit 3
        fi
        sleep 0.5  # Simulate work
        echo "{\"success\":true,\"condition\":\"idle\"}"
        ;;
    --robot-activity=*)
        if [[ "$scenario" == "rate_limited" ]]; then
            echo "{\"success\":true,\"agents\":[{\"state\":\"WAITING\",\"rate_limited\":true}]}"
        else
            echo "{\"success\":true,\"agents\":[{\"state\":\"WAITING\",\"rate_limited\":false}]}"
        fi
        ;;
    kill)
        echo "killed"
        ;;
esac
```

## Simulated Pane Output

For E2E tests, we need to simulate what tmux capture-pane would return.
Create scripts/test_bin/tmux that produces mock output:

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# Mock tmux capture-pane

case "$scenario" in
    ok)
        cat << 'EOF'
I have read the AGENTS.md and README.md files carefully.

RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_BEGIN
{"summary":"Test repo for agent-sweep","conventions":["Bash 4.0+"],"risks":[],"notes":[]}
RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_END

Based on my analysis, here is the commit plan:

RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{
  "commits": [
    {"files": ["modified.txt"], "message": "fix: update test file\n\nUpdated the test file content."}
  ],
  "push": false,
  "excluded_files": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "risks": []
}
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_END
EOF
        ;;
    ok_with_release)
        cat << 'EOF'
[... understanding and commit output ...]

RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{
  "version": "1.1.0",
  "tag": "v1.1.0",
  "changelog_entry": "## v1.1.0\n\n- Fixed test file",
  "version_files": [{"path": "VERSION", "old": "1.0.0", "new": "1.1.0"}],
  "checks": ["tests"]
}
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_END
EOF
        ;;
    invalid_json)
        cat << 'EOF'
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{not valid json
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_END
EOF
        ;;
    no_markers)
        echo "Agent did not produce expected markers"
        ;;
esac
```

## Test Setup

```bash
setup_ntm_mock() {
    mkdir -p "$TEST_BIN"
    
    # Create mock ntm script
    cat > "$TEST_BIN/ntm" << 'MOCK'
    [script contents]
    MOCK
    chmod +x "$TEST_BIN/ntm"
    
    # Create mock tmux script
    cat > "$TEST_BIN/tmux" << 'MOCK'
    [script contents]
    MOCK
    chmod +x "$TEST_BIN/tmux"
    
    export PATH="$TEST_BIN:$PATH"
}
```

## Scenario Coverage

| Scenario | Tests | Expected Behavior |
|----------|-------|-------------------|
| ok | Basic workflow | Full success with commits |
| ok_with_release | Release workflow | Commits + release |
| timeout | Timeout handling | Exit 1, state saved |
| resource_busy | Session conflict | Retry or fail |
| agent_error | Agent crash | Exit 1, artifacts captured |
| rate_limited | API limits | Backoff triggered |
| spawn_fail | ntm error | Exit 3 (dependency) |
| invalid_json | Bad output | Validation failure |
| no_markers | Missing markers | Plan extraction failure |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mock produces valid JSON for all ntm robot commands
- [ ] Scenario variable controls failure modes
- [ ] Mock tmux produces commit plan with proper markers
- [ ] Release plan scenario available for Phase 3 tests
- [ ] Invalid JSON scenario tests validation
- [ ] No markers scenario tests extraction error handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:55:51.029250138-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:29:13.855611086-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:29:13.855611086-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wu0c","depends_on_id":"bd-hnbf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:55:57.146933577-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-xcj6","title":"Implement JSON output mode for review command","description":"# Task: Implement JSON Output Mode

## Purpose
Add --json flag to `ru review` for machine-readable output, following the ru convention of structured stdout and human-readable stderr.

## Background: ru Output Convention
From AGENTS.md:
- stderr: All human-readable output (progress, errors, summary, help)
- stdout: Only structured output (JSON in --json mode, paths otherwise)

## JSON Output Schema

### Discovery Output (--dry-run --json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "discovery",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "summary": {
    "repos_scanned": 50,
    "items_found": 23,
    "by_priority": {"critical": 2, "high": 5, "normal": 12, "low": 4},
    "by_type": {"issues": 18, "prs": 5}
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "repo": "owner/repo",
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Bug in auth",
      "priority": "high",
      "score": 85,
      "labels": ["bug", "security"],
      "created_at": "2024-12-15T...",
      "updated_at": "2025-01-01T..."
    }
  ]
}
```

### Session Status Output (--json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "plan",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "sessions": {
    "owner/repo1": {
      "state": "generating",
      "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "items_processing": 3
    },
    "owner/repo2": {
      "state": "waiting",
      "wait_reason": "ask_user_question",
      "question_id": "q123"
    }
  },
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q123",
      "repo": "owner/repo2",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor?",
      "options": ["Yes", "No", "Skip"],
      "waiting_since": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "progress": {
    "sessions_total": 4,
    "sessions_complete": 1,
    "sessions_active": 2,
    "sessions_pending": 1,
    "questions_pending": 1,
    "questions_answered": 3
  }
}
```

### Completion Output (--json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "plan",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "complete",
  "exit_code": 0,
  "summary": {
    "repos_reviewed": 4,
    "items_processed": 12,
    "items_fixed": 8,
    "items_skipped": 3,
    "items_needs_info": 1,
    "commits_created": 15,
    "questions_asked": 5,
    "duration_seconds": 847
  },
  "repos": {
    "owner/repo1": {
      "status": "complete",
      "plan_file": "/path/to/.ru/review-plan.json",
      "commits": 4,
      "items_processed": 3
    }
  }
}
```

### Apply Output (--apply --json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "apply",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "complete",
  "applied": {
    "owner/repo1": {
      "pushed": true,
      "commits": 4,
      "gh_actions_executed": [
        {"op": "comment", "target": "issue#42", "success": true},
        {"op": "close", "target": "issue#42", "success": true}
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

## Implementation

### JSON Mode Flag
```bash
# In parse_review_args()
--json) REVIEW_JSON_OUTPUT=true ;;
```

### Output Router
```bash
review_output() {
    local type="$1"
    local data="$2"
    
    if [[ "$REVIEW_JSON_OUTPUT" == "true" ]]; then
        echo "$data"  # stdout for JSON
    else
        echo "$data" >&2  # stderr for human
    fi
}
```

### JSON Builders
```bash
build_discovery_json() { ... }
build_status_json() { ... }
build_completion_json() { ... }
build_apply_json() { ... }
```

## Integration Points
- Discovery phase outputs items JSON
- Session monitor outputs status JSON on refresh
- Completion outputs summary JSON
- Apply phase outputs actions JSON

## Testing
- Verify JSON is valid (jq parses it)
- Verify stderr has human output in --json mode
- Verify stdout is empty without --json
- Verify all schema fields present

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --json flag accepted
- [ ] Discovery JSON output correct
- [ ] Status JSON output correct
- [ ] Completion JSON output correct
- [ ] Apply JSON output correct
- [ ] Human output goes to stderr in JSON mode

 {"id":"bd-xdrt","title":"Phase 3: ntm Integration (Robot Mode, Activity Detection)","description":"Phase 3: ntm Integration

Overview
--------
Integrate with ntm (Named Tmux Manager) robot mode for advanced orchestration
capabilities: activity detection, health monitoring, and question routing.

Why ntm?
--------
ntm provides capabilities beyond basic tmux:
- Velocity-based activity detection (chars/sec)
- Health monitoring with error pattern detection
- Robot mode JSON API for programmatic control
- Workflow pipelines for complex multi-step tasks
- Session management and routing

Components
----------

3.1 ntm Driver Implementation
   Implement the unified driver interface using ntm robot mode API:
   - ntm --robot-spawn: Create sessions
   - ntm --robot-status: Query session state
   - ntm --robot-health: Monitor session health
   - ntm --robot-route: Get best pane for new work
   - ntm --robot-send: Deliver messages to sessions

3.2 Activity State Detection
   ntm detects Claude state via output velocity:
   - GENERATING: velocity > 10 chars/sec (active output)
   - WAITING: velocity < 1 chars/sec + prompt pattern
   - THINKING: low velocity, no prompt
   - STALLED: velocity == 0 for > 30 seconds
   - ERROR: error patterns detected

3.3 Wait Reason Extraction
   When WAITING, determine WHY:
   - ask_user_question: AskUserQuestion tool detected
   - agent_question_text: Question in plain text
   - external_prompt: Git/SSH/auth prompt
   - unknown: At prompt, reason unclear

3.4 Health Monitoring
   Monitor session health continuously:
   - Detect rate limits (429, quota exceeded)
   - Detect crashes (panic, SIGSEGV)
   - Detect stalls (no output for extended period)
   - Trigger recovery actions

3.5 Workflow Pipeline
   Define github-review.yaml workflow:
   - verify_prerequisites: Check gh auth
   - understand_codebase: Create/update digest
   - review_issues_prs: Main review work
   - finalize_artifacts: Validate plan created

3.6 Rate-Limit Governor
   Adaptive concurrency based on real limits:
   - Query GitHub API for remaining quota
   - Detect model rate limits from 429s
   - Adjust parallelism dynamically
   - Circuit breaker for cascading failures

Exit Criteria
-------------
- ntm driver passes all interface tests
- Activity detection works reliably
- Wait reasons extracted correctly
- Health monitoring catches common issues
- Rate-limit governor adjusts concurrency

Estimated Effort
----------------
~400 lines Bash + ~300 lines in ntm (Go)
Note: ntm changes are separate project","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:42.232591922-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:33:16.238846656-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:33:16.238846656-05:00","close_reason":"All ru-side components implemented:
- 3.1 ntm Driver: Full robot mode API (spawn, status, send, activity, health) at lines 5534-5810
- 3.2 Activity Detection: GENERATING/WAITING/THINKING/STALLED with velocity-based detection
- 3.3 Wait Reason Extraction: detect_wait_reason() with ask_user_question/external_prompt/agent_question_text
- 3.4 Health Monitoring: ntm driver reports health_monitoring=true, uses --robot-health
- 3.6 Rate-Limit Governor: Complete implementation (5869-6165) with circuit breaker, GitHub quota tracking, model rate limit detection

Note: 3.5 Workflow Pipeline (github-review.yaml) is explicitly ntm-side (Go) per phase description. Out of scope for ru.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdrt","depends_on_id":"bd-5yy3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:26.666656889-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
